learning_objectives: Understand the academic requirements, grading criteria, and deliverables for the internship course
Set professional development goals aligned with career aspirations
Establish communication protocols with faculty advisor and site supervisor

lecture_notes.introduction: This course bridges your academic training and professional career. Your internship is a real job with real expectations, and this weekly seminar provides structure for reflecting on your experience and building professional skills alongside it.
key_concepts: Internship requirements: minimum 20 hours/week at an approved game industry site
Weekly journal expectations: structured reflection, not diary entries
Communication protocol: bi-weekly check-ins with faculty advisor, regular updates to supervisor
Professional development goal-setting using the SMART framework
Ethical obligations: NDA compliance, professional conduct, representing the university
lecture_notes.demonstration: Goal-setting workshop: each student drafts three SMART professional development goals for their internship and receives peer and faculty feedback
setup_requirements: Signed internship agreement on file with the department
Contact information for site supervisor submitted to faculty advisor
Digital journal set up (Google Docs, Notion, or similar) shared with faculty advisor

review.value: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound objectives that transform vague aspirations into trackable professional development targets
Non-disclosure agreements are standard in the game industry; all course deliverables must be reviewed for compliance before submission, and sensitive details should be generalized

learning_objectives: Navigate the first weeks of a professional game studio environment
Apply effective communication strategies for remote, hybrid, and in-person work settings
Identify and adapt to studio culture, processes, and tools
readings: Schreier, Press Reset, Ch. 1-2 (on studio culture and adaptation)
Harvard Business Review, 'How to Make the Most of Your Internship'

lecture_notes.introduction: Your first weeks at a studio set the tone for your entire internship. Being proactive, asking good questions, and adapting quickly to the studio's workflow are more important than demonstrating technical skill in the early days.
key_concepts: Onboarding strategies: learning the codebase, tools, and pipeline without blocking others
Asking effective questions: when to self-research vs. when to ask, how to frame questions for senior developers
Communication channels: Slack/Teams etiquette, standup participation, status updates
Imposter syndrome awareness: recognizing it, managing it, and performing despite it
Building relationships with mentors and colleagues
lecture_notes.demonstration: Round-table discussion: students share their first-week experiences, challenges encountered, and strategies that worked, with faculty facilitating problem-solving for common issues
setup_requirements: Initial observations documented in weekly journal

review.value: Try to solve a problem yourself for five minutes before asking for help, but never struggle alone for more than thirty minutes; your time and your colleague's time are both valuable
Proactively communicating what you accomplished, what you are working on, and what is blocking you builds trust and prevents surprises for your supervisor

class_schedule.topic: Technical Skills in Practice: Bridging Academy and Industry
learning_objectives: Identify gaps between academic training and professional practice in game development
Develop strategies for rapidly learning new tools, engines, and pipelines on the job
Document technical learning for portfolio and career development
readings: Game Programming Patterns, Introduction (Robert Nystrom) - on code in production contexts
Selected blog posts on transitioning from student to professional game developer (provided on LMS)

lecture_notes.introduction: Every intern discovers areas where their academic training prepared them well and areas where professional practice differs significantly. Recognizing and closing those gaps quickly is a skill in itself.
key_concepts: Common academic-to-industry gaps: version control at scale, code review culture, legacy codebases
Learning strategies for unfamiliar engines, frameworks, and proprietary tools
Production code standards: readability, documentation, testing expectations in professional settings
Asking for code reviews and incorporating feedback productively
Documenting your learning process for portfolio case studies
lecture_notes.demonstration: Skills gap analysis workshop: each student maps their academic skills against their internship role requirements, identifies specific gaps, and creates a learning plan with resources and timelines
setup_requirements: List of tools and technologies used at internship site
Self-assessment of current skill levels in each area

review.value: Professional code reviews are the fastest way to improve; actively requesting reviews and reading other developers' reviews teaches studio conventions and advanced techniques

Most professional game development involves working with existing codebases; the ability to read, understand, and safely modify code written by others is more valuable than writing new code from scratch

learning_objectives: Assess progress toward professional development goals established at the start of the internship
Articulate the most significant professional lessons learned so far
Adjust goals and strategies for the second half of the internship
readings: Dweck, Mindset, Ch. 3 - on growth mindset in professional contexts
Mid-term reflection prompt document (provided on LMS)

lecture_notes.introduction: The midpoint of your internship is the time to honestly assess where you are, what you have learned, and what you want to prioritize in the remaining weeks. This is also your opportunity to discuss your mid-term reflection with peers and faculty.
key_concepts: Goal progress review: measuring against SMART objectives set in week 1
Identifying unexpected learning: skills and insights you did not anticipate gaining
Adjusting goals: what to prioritize in the second half based on experience
Soliciting feedback from your supervisor: how to ask for and receive constructive criticism
Networking progress: relationships built and professional connections established
lecture_notes.demonstration: Mid-term presentation: each student presents a 5-minute summary of their internship experience, key learnings, and adjusted goals, followed by faculty and peer feedback
setup_requirements: Mid-term reflection essay completed and submitted before class

review.value: Asking your supervisor 'What is one thing I could do better?' is more productive than 'How am I doing?' because it invites specific, actionable feedback rather than general reassurance
Adjusting your goals based on experience is a sign of growth, not failure; rigid adherence to initial goals ignores what you have learned about yourself and the industry

class_schedule.topic: Supervisor Evaluation and Professional Growth Documentation
learning_objectives: Prepare for and participate in a formal supervisor evaluation process
Translate internship accomplishments into portfolio-ready documentation
Identify transferable skills developed during the internship
readings: NACE (National Association of Colleges and Employers) internship competency framework
Portfolio documentation guide for internship work (provided on LMS)

lecture_notes.introduction: Your supervisor evaluation provides external validation of your professional contributions. Preparing for this evaluation and documenting your work appropriately ensures your internship experience translates into career assets.
key_concepts: Supervisor evaluation form: preparing your supervisor with context and specific examples of your contributions
Self-evaluation alignment: comparing your self-assessment with supervisor feedback
Portfolio documentation under NDA: generalizing specific work into demonstrable skills
Transferable skills inventory: communication, problem-solving, collaboration, adaptability
LinkedIn and professional profile updates based on internship experience
lecture_notes.demonstration: Portfolio documentation workshop: students practice writing NDA-compliant case studies of their internship work, focusing on skills demonstrated and problems solved rather than proprietary details
setup_requirements: Supervisor evaluation form distributed to site supervisor at least one week prior
List of internship accomplishments and contributions prepared

review.value: Describe the type of problem you solved, the skills you applied, and the impact of your work without revealing proprietary mechanics, code, or unannounced features
Skills like 'debugging complex systems under time pressure' or 'communicating technical constraints to non-technical stakeholders' transfer across studios and even across industries

learning_objectives: Deliver a professional presentation summarizing the internship experience and outcomes
Articulate a clear post-graduation career strategy informed by internship experience
Provide constructive feedback to peers on their internship presentations
readings: Review all weekly journal entries in preparation for synthesis

lecture_notes.introduction: Your final presentation synthesizes fifteen weeks of professional experience into a cohesive narrative. This is practice for the conversations you will have in future interviews when asked about your industry experience.
key_concepts: Presentation structure: context, contributions, challenges, growth, career impact
Storytelling in professional contexts: turning daily work into a compelling narrative
Quantifying contributions where possible: bugs fixed, features shipped, tools built
Lessons learned for the next role: what you will seek out and what you will avoid
Maintaining professional relationships after the internship ends
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each student delivers a 10-minute final presentation to faculty and peers, followed by 5 minutes of Q&A. Faculty provide feedback on both content and presentation skills.
setup_requirements: Presentation materials prepared (slides, demo reel, or portfolio walkthrough)
Thank-you notes drafted for internship supervisor and colleagues

review.value: Frame your internship as a growth narrative with a clear arc: what you knew coming in, what challenged you, how you rose to those challenges, and how the experience shaped your professional direction

The professional relationships you built during your internship are career assets; a brief thank-you note and occasional check-ins maintain the connection for future references, collaborations, or job leads

major_assignments.title: Internship Plan
major_assignments.description: Develop a structured internship plan that defines your professional development goals, establishes communication protocols with your supervisor and faculty advisor, and outlines what you intend to learn and contribute during the internship.
objectives: Define three to five SMART professional development goals for the internship
Establish clear communication protocols with supervisor and faculty advisor
Research the studio's products, culture, and technology stack
Create a learning plan for tools and skills required by the role
deliverables: Internship plan document (4-5 pages) with SMART goals, communication plan, studio research, and learning objectives
Skills gap analysis mapping current abilities against role requirements
Signed internship agreement and supervisor contact information
Initial weekly journal entry documenting expectations and first impressions

criteria: Goals are specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound
Goals reflect genuine professional development aspirations
Goals are informed by research into the studio and role
At least one goal addresses a known skill gap
Communication protocols are clearly defined and realistic
Studio research demonstrates genuine effort beyond surface-level information
Skills gap analysis is honest and specific
Learning plan includes concrete resources and timelines
Document is well-written and professionally formatted
All required forms and agreements are completed
Initial journal entry is reflective and substantive
Plan demonstrates mature professional expectations
Well-defined SMART goals demonstrating genuine professional development intent
Thorough preparation with studio research and skills gap analysis
Professional documentation quality throughout all deliverables
Clear communication plan establishing expectations with all parties

major_assignments.title: Mid-Term Reflection
major_assignments.description: Write a comprehensive mid-term reflection assessing your progress toward professional development goals, documenting key learnings, and adjusting your plan for the second half of the internship. Include specific examples from your work experience.
objectives: Assess progress toward each SMART goal with specific evidence
Identify the most significant professional lessons learned during the first half
Document unexpected challenges and how they were addressed
Revise goals and strategies for the remaining weeks based on experience
deliverables: Mid-term reflection essay (5-7 pages) with goal progress assessment, key learnings, and revised plan
Updated SMART goals document with revisions highlighted and justified
Summary of supervisor feedback received (formal or informal)
5-minute in-class presentation on internship experience to date

criteria: Honest and specific assessment of progress toward each goal
Evidence and examples support all claims of growth or challenge
Unexpected learning and challenges are documented thoughtfully
Self-awareness is demonstrated through balanced reflection
Revised goals reflect lessons learned from the first half
Revisions are justified with specific reasoning
New goals remain SMART and achievable within remaining time
Strategy adjustments are practical and actionable
Reflection connects daily tasks to broader professional development
Supervisor feedback is integrated thoughtfully
Industry observations demonstrate growing professional awareness
Lessons learned are generalizable beyond the specific internship
Presentation is clear, concise, and within the time limit
Key learnings are communicated effectively to peers
Student responds thoughtfully to questions
Presentation demonstrates genuine engagement with the experience
Honest and evidence-based self-assessment of professional growth
Thoughtful goal revision demonstrating learning and adaptability
Professional insight connecting internship experience to career development
Effective communication of experience through writing and presentation

major_assignments.title: Supervisor Evaluation Form
major_assignments.description: Coordinate with your site supervisor to complete a formal evaluation of your internship performance. Submit the completed evaluation along with a self-evaluation and a written comparison analyzing the alignment between the two assessments.
objectives: Facilitate a formal evaluation process with your site supervisor
Complete an honest self-evaluation using the same criteria
Analyze the alignment and gaps between supervisor and self-evaluation
Demonstrate professional maturity in receiving and processing feedback
deliverables: Completed supervisor evaluation form (standardized form provided by the department)
Self-evaluation using the same criteria and rating scale
Comparative analysis (2-3 pages) examining alignment and gaps between evaluations
Action items for the final weeks based on evaluation findings

criteria: Supervisor evaluation is complete with all sections filled out
Evaluation was submitted on time, demonstrating proactive coordination
Supervisor comments provide substantive detail beyond checkbox ratings
Self-evaluation is complete and uses the same rating criteria
Analysis identifies specific areas of alignment and divergence
Divergences are explored with genuine curiosity rather than defensiveness
Patterns across evaluation categories are identified and discussed
Analysis demonstrates self-awareness and professional maturity
Action items are specific and achievable in the remaining weeks
Actions directly address areas identified for improvement
Plan acknowledges strengths to maintain alongside areas to develop
Timeline for action items is realistic
Complete evaluation package submitted on time through proactive coordination
Insightful comparative analysis demonstrating self-awareness
Practical action plan leveraging feedback for final weeks
Professional maturity in receiving and processing evaluation

major_assignments.title: Final Internship Report and Presentation
major_assignments.description: Produce a comprehensive final report synthesizing your entire internship experience, including professional growth documentation, portfolio additions, and career impact assessment. Deliver a formal presentation to faculty summarizing your internship journey.
objectives: Synthesize the full internship experience into a coherent professional narrative
Document specific contributions and portfolio-worthy work (NDA-compliant)
Assess the internship's impact on career direction and professional readiness
Deliver a polished presentation demonstrating professional communication skills
deliverables: Final report (8-12 pages) covering contributions, growth, challenges, and career impact
Portfolio additions: at least two NDA-compliant case studies or work samples from the internship
10-minute formal presentation to faculty with Q&A
Thank-you correspondence drafted for supervisor and key colleagues

criteria: Comprehensive narrative covering the full internship arc
Specific contributions are documented with evidence of impact
Challenges and failures are discussed honestly with lessons learned
Career impact assessment is thoughtful and grounded in experience
Writing quality is professional and suitable for a hiring manager audience
At least two substantive case studies or work samples included
NDA compliance is maintained throughout
Work is presented in a portfolio-ready format
Skills demonstrated and problems solved are clearly articulated
Presentation is well-structured, engaging, and within time limit
Key contributions and growth are communicated effectively
Visual aids support the narrative without overwhelming it
Q&A responses demonstrate deep reflection on the experience
Resume is updated and accurately reflects internship experience
Thank-you correspondence is professional and personalized
All deliverables are proofread and error-free
Overall package demonstrates readiness for professional employment
Comprehensive final report synthesizing a rich internship experience
Portfolio-ready work samples demonstrating professional contributions
Polished presentation showcasing communication skills and self-awareness
Professional package demonstrating genuine career readiness

internship_performance.description: Supervisor evaluation of work quality and professionalism

weekly_journals.description: Weekly reflection journals documenting learning and growth

final_report.description: Comprehensive internship report with portfolio additions

presentation.description: Final presentation to faculty summarizing internship experience

course_policies.attendance: Attendance at the weekly Friday seminar is mandatory. More than 2 unexcused absences will result in grade reduction. Internship site attendance follows the schedule agreed upon with your supervisor.
course_policies.late_submissions: Weekly journals are due every Friday by 5 PM. Late journals are penalized 10% per day. Major assignment deadlines are firm; extensions require advance arrangement with the faculty advisor.
course_policies.collaboration: All course deliverables are individual work reflecting your personal internship experience. Peer feedback during seminar discussions is encouraged and expected.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All written work must be your own. Internship work documented in reports and portfolio must comply with your NDA. When in doubt about what can be shared, consult your site supervisor before including it in any deliverable.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Students need access to a digital journal platform, document editing software, and presentation tools. All deliverables must be submitted through the LMS. Communication with the faculty advisor requires reliable email and video conferencing access.
